title: new WebSocket with rejectUnauthorized: false — the wss:// connection skips TLS certificate validation (MITM)

params.passDetail: The WebSocket keeps certificate validation on (rejectUnauthorized is not false) — the wss:// server's TLS identity is verified.
params.failDetail: new WebSocket is created with rejectUnauthorized: false, disabling TLS certificate validation on the wss:// connection. A network attacker can present a forged certificate and man-in-the-middle the real-time channel — reading and injecting messages (auth tokens, trades, commands). Remove rejectUnauthorized: false; pass a proper `ca` if a private CA is required.
params.absentCallDetail: No WebSocket construction in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: WebSocket does not set rejectUnauthorized; the secure default (true) applies.

provenance.note: rejectUnauthorized: false on a ws client is the WebSocket version of the https bypass — pasted to connect to a dev server with a self-signed cert, it ships and exposes the real-time channel (often carrying auth tokens or trading data) to MITM.
